title: lophiolepis ligularis subsp. armata
intro: Lophiolepis ligularis subsp. armata, also known as the spiny club moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the genus Lophiolepis and is characterized by its spiny branches and small, scale-like leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Lophiolepis ligularis subsp. armata include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a decoction to harness its medicinal properties.
